timeline.title: Employer Preparation
timeline.description: Your employer prepares to sponsor you. Discuss H-1B plans with your manager/HR.

timeline.title: Registration Period Opens
timeline.description: USCIS opens electronic registration (usually first week of March for ~14 days). Employer registers you online ($10 fee per registration).

timeline.title: Lottery Selection ⭐
timeline.description: USCIS conducts lottery and notifies selected registrations. Not everyone gets selected!

timeline.title: Petition Filing (If Selected)
timeline.description: If selected, employer has 90 days to file full H-1B petition with USCIS (filing fee: ~$2,500-$5,000+).

timeline.title: USCIS Processing
timeline.description: USCIS reviews petition. May request additional evidence (RFE). Processing can take 2-6+ months (premium processing available for faster decision).

timeline.title: H-1B Start Date 🎉
timeline.description: If approved, your H-1B status begins! This is the earliest you can start working on H-1B.

process.title: Find an H-1B Sponsor
process.description: Your employer must be willing and able to sponsor your H-1B visa.
points: Discuss sponsorship early in interview/employment
Many large companies have H-1B programs
Employer bears most filing costs
Some cap-exempt employers (universities, nonprofits) have no lottery

process.title: Labor Condition Application (LCA)
process.description: Employer files LCA with Department of Labor to certify wages and working conditions.
points: Must pay prevailing wage for your position
DOL approval typically takes 7-10 days
Required before H-1B filing
Posted at workplace for employee notice

process.title: Registration & Lottery (March)
process.description: Employer registers you during the registration period. USCIS conducts random lottery.
points: Electronic registration only ($10 fee)
Can be registered by multiple employers (increases odds)
Master's degree holders get two lottery chances
Selection doesn't guarantee approval

process.title: Petition Filing (If Selected)
process.description: If selected, employer files Form I-129 with supporting documents and fees.
points: Must file within 90 days of selection
Include degree certificates, job description, LCA
Premium processing available (~$2,805 for 15-day decision)
Regular processing: 2-6 months or more

process.title: USCIS Adjudication
process.description: USCIS reviews the petition. They may issue a Request for Evidence (RFE).
points: Respond to RFEs promptly and thoroughly
Approval results in I-797 notice
Denial can be appealed in some cases
Premium processing guarantees 15-day response

process.title: H-1B Status Begins (Oct 1)
process.description: Upon approval, your H-1B status begins on October 1. Cap-gap extends OPT until then.
points: Valid for initial 3-year period
Can be extended for another 3 years
Allows dual intent (can apply for green card)
Must work for sponsoring employer only

requirements.title: Education
items: Degree must be related to the specialty occupation
Foreign degrees must be evaluated for US equivalency
Some experience can substitute for education (3 years work = 1 year study)

requirements.title: Specialty Occupation
items: Job requires theoretical and practical application of specialized knowledge
Position normally requires bachelor's degree minimum
Common fields: IT, Engineering, Science, Healthcare, Finance, Architecture
Job duties must match your degree field

requirements.title: Employer Requirements
items: Must be US employer (or US operations)
Must pay prevailing wage for the position
Must file LCA and maintain compliance
